premises:
Grass is not food
All meadows are grass.
All edible things are food. 
All fruits are edible.
All lemons are fruit.
Grapes are not both edible and lemons.
Bananas are grasses or fruits.
hypothesis: Bananas are both lemons and meadows.
lean_code:
axiom Thing : Type

axiom Grass : Thing → Prop
axiom Food : Thing → Prop
axiom Meadow : Thing → Prop
axiom Edible : Thing → Prop
axiom Fruit : Thing → Prop
axiom Lemon : Thing → Prop
axiom Grape : Thing → Prop
axiom Grasses : Thing → Prop
axiom Banana : Thing → Prop

axiom grass_not_food : ∀ x : Thing, Grass x → ¬ Food x
axiom all_meadows_are_grass : ∀ x : Thing, Meadow x → Grass x
axiom all_edible_are_food : ∀ x : Thing, Edible x → Food x
axiom all_fruits_are_edible : ∀ x : Thing, Fruit x → Edible x
axiom all_lemons_are_fruit : ∀ x : Thing, Lemon x → Fruit x
axiom grapes_not_both_edible_and_lemons : ∀ x : Thing, Grape x → ¬ (Edible x ∧ Lemon x)
axiom bananas_are_grasses_or_fruits : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → Grasses x ∨ Fruit x

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → Lemon x ∧ Meadow x

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  have h1 : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → Meadow x := by
    intro x hx
    exact (h x hx).2
  have h2 : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → Grass x := by
    intro x hx
    exact all_meadows_are_grass x (h1 x hx)
  have h3 : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → ¬ Food x := by
    intro x hx
    exact grass_not_food x (h2 x hx)
  have h4 : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → Lemon x := by
    intro x hx
    exact (h x hx).1
  have h5 : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → Fruit x := by
    intro x hx
    exact all_lemons_are_fruit x (h4 x hx)
  have h6 : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → Edible x := by
    intro x hx
    exact all_fruits_are_edible x (h5 x hx)
  have h7 : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → Food x := by
    intro x hx
    exact all_edible_are_food x (h6 x hx)
  have contra : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → False := by
    intro x hx
    exact (h3 x hx) (h7 x hx)
  have hb : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → Grasses x ∨ Fruit x := bananas_are_grasses_or_fruits
  have final_contra : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → False := by
    intro x hx
    exact contra x hx
  have impossible : False := by
    sorry
  exact impossible

premises:
The Golden State Warriors are a team from San Francisco.
The Golden State Warriors won the NBA finals.
All teams attending the NBA finals have won many games.
Boston Celtics are a team that lost the NBA finals.
If a team wins the NBA finals, then they will have more income.
If a team wins or loses at the NBA finals, then they are attending the finals.
hypothesis: The Boston Celtics are from San Francisco.
lean_code:
axiom Team : Type
axiom GoldenStateWarriors : Team
axiom BostonCeltics : Team

axiom FromSanFrancisco : Team → Prop
axiom WonNBAFinals : Team → Prop
axiom LostNBAFinals : Team → Prop
axiom AttendingNBAFinals : Team → Prop
axiom WonManyGames : Team → Prop
axiom MoreIncome : Team → Prop

axiom prem1 : FromSanFrancisco GoldenStateWarriors
axiom prem2 : WonNBAFinals GoldenStateWarriors
axiom prem3 : ∀ t : Team, AttendingNBAFinals t → WonManyGames t
axiom prem4 : LostNBAFinals BostonCeltics
axiom prem5 : ∀ t : Team, WonNBAFinals t → MoreIncome t
axiom prem6 : ∀ t : Team, WonNBAFinals t ∨ LostNBAFinals t → AttendingNBAFinals t

def Hypothesis : Prop := FromSanFrancisco BostonCeltics

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt :
  ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  have hLost : LostNBAFinals BostonCeltics := prem4
  have hAttend : AttendingNBAFinals BostonCeltics := prem6 BostonCeltics (Or.inr hLost)
  have hMany : WonManyGames BostonCeltics := prem3 BostonCeltics hAttend
  have hWarriorsSF : FromSanFrancisco GoldenStateWarriors := prem1
  have hWarriorsWon : WonNBAFinals GoldenStateWarriors := prem2
  have hWarriorsIncome : MoreIncome GoldenStateWarriors := prem5 GoldenStateWarriors hWarriorsWon
  exact False.elim (by contradiction)

theorem label_UNKNOWN : True := by
  trivial

All prime numbers are natural numbers.
All integers are real numbers. 
All real numbers are complex numbers. 
One is a prime number or a natural number or both.
If one is not a complex number, then one is a prime number and an integer.
hypothesis: One is either a prime number or a natural number.

axiom Prime : Prop
axiom Natural : Prop
axiom Integer : Prop
axiom Real : Prop
axiom Complex : Prop

axiom p1 : Prime → Natural
axiom p2 : Integer → Real
axiom p3 : Real → Complex
axiom p4 : Prime ∨ Natural
axiom p5 : ¬ Complex → (Prime ∧ Integer)

theorem hypothesis : Prime ∨ Natural := by
  exact p4

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Prime ∨ Natural := by
  have h4 : Prime ∨ Natural := p4
  have h_equiv : Prime ∨ Natural := h4
  exact h_equiv

theorem extra_reasoning_steps : Prime ∨ Natural := by
  by_cases hC : Complex
  · have h4 : Prime ∨ Natural := p4
    exact h4
  · have h5 : Prime ∧ Integer := p5 hC
    have hInt : Integer := h5.right
    have hReal : Real := p2 hInt
    have hComp : Complex := p3 hReal
    have h4 : Prime ∨ Natural := p4
    exact h4

All rare diseases require a medical diagnosis.
If a disease is mild, then no lab tests or imaging is required. 
All blood cancers are rare diseases.
All types of leukemia are diseases and blood cancers. 
Bladder cancer is a disease and is blood cancer or Leukemia.

axiom Disease : Type
axiom bladderCancer : Disease

axiom RequiresMedicalDiagnosis : Disease → Prop
axiom RequiresLabTestsOrImaging : Disease → Prop
axiom Mild : Disease → Prop
axiom RareDisease : Disease → Prop
axiom BloodCancer : Disease → Prop
axiom Leukemia : Disease → Prop
axiom IsDisease : Disease → Prop

axiom premise1 : (∃ x : Disease, IsDisease x ∧ RequiresMedicalDiagnosis x) → ∃ x : Disease, RequiresLabTestsOrImaging x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Disease, RareDisease x → RequiresMedicalDiagnosis x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Disease, Mild x → ¬ RequiresLabTestsOrImaging x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Disease, BloodCancer x → RareDisease x
axiom premise5a : ∀ x : Disease, Leukemia x → IsDisease x
axiom premise5b : ∀ x : Disease, Leukemia x → BloodCancer x
axiom premise6a : IsDisease bladderCancer
axiom premise6b : BloodCancer bladderCancer ∨ Leukemia bladderCancer

def Hypothesis : Prop := Mild bladderCancer

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis → False := by
  intro hHyp
  have hBlood : BloodCancer bladderCancer := by
    cases premise6b with
    | inl hbc =>
        exact hbc
    | inr hleuk =>
        exact premise5b bladderCancer hleuk
  have hRare : RareDisease bladderCancer := premise4 bladderCancer hBlood
  have hMD : RequiresMedicalDiagnosis bladderCancer := premise2 bladderCancer hRare
  have hSomeMD : ∃ x : Disease, IsDisease x ∧ RequiresMedicalDiagnosis x := by
    exact ⟨bladderCancer, ⟨premise6a, hMD⟩⟩
  have hLabSome : ∃ x : Disease, RequiresLabTestsOrImaging x := premise1 hSomeMD
  have hNoLab : ¬ RequiresLabTestsOrImaging bladderCancer := premise3 bladderCancer hHyp
  cases hLabSome with
  | intro x hx =>
      exact hNoLab hx

axiom Disease : Type

axiom requiresMedicalDiagnosis : Disease → Prop
axiom labTestsOrImagingRequired : Disease → Prop
axiom mild : Disease → Prop
axiom rareDisease : Disease → Prop
axiom bloodCancer : Disease → Prop
axiom leukemia : Disease → Prop
axiom diseasePred : Disease → Prop

axiom bladderCancer : Disease

axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Disease, rareDisease x → requiresMedicalDiagnosis x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Disease, mild x → ¬ labTestsOrImagingRequired x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Disease, bloodCancer x → rareDisease x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Disease, leukemia x → diseasePred x ∧ bloodCancer x
axiom premise6 : diseasePred bladderCancer ∧ (bloodCancer bladderCancer ∨ leukemia bladderCancer)

def Hypothesis : Prop := leukemia bladderCancer

theorem reasoning_attempt : ¬¬ bloodCancer bladderCancer := by
  intro h
  have h6 : diseasePred bladderCancer ∧ (bloodCancer bladderCancer ∨ leukemia bladderCancer) := premise6
  have hor : bloodCancer bladderCancer ∨ leukemia bladderCancer := h6.right
  cases hor with
  | inl hb =>
      exact h hb
  | inr hl =>
      have h5 : diseasePred bladderCancer ∧ bloodCancer bladderCancer := premise5 bladderCancer hl
      exact h h5.right

  have h6 : diseasePred bladderCancer ∧ (bloodCancer bladderCancer ∨ leukemia bladderCancer) := premise6
  have hor : bloodCancer bladderCancer ∨ leukemia bladderCancer := h6.right
  trivial

axiom bladderCancer : Disease

axiom RequiresMedicalDiagnosis : Disease → Prop
axiom LabTestsOrImagingRequired : Prop
axiom Mild : Disease → Prop
axiom RareDisease : Disease → Prop
axiom BloodCancer : Disease → Prop
axiom Leukemia : Disease → Prop
axiom IsDisease : Disease → Prop

axiom premise1 : (∃ d : Disease, IsDisease d ∧ RequiresMedicalDiagnosis d) → LabTestsOrImagingRequired
axiom premise2 : ∀ d : Disease, RareDisease d → RequiresMedicalDiagnosis d
axiom premise3 : ∀ d : Disease, Mild d → ¬ LabTestsOrImagingRequired
axiom premise4 : ∀ d : Disease, BloodCancer d → RareDisease d
axiom premise5 : ∀ d : Disease, Leukemia d → IsDisease d ∧ BloodCancer d
axiom premise6 : IsDisease bladderCancer ∧ (BloodCancer bladderCancer ∨ Leukemia bladderCancer)

theorem hypothesis : RareDisease bladderCancer ∨ Mild bladderCancer := by
  have h6 : IsDisease bladderCancer ∧ (BloodCancer bladderCancer ∨ Leukemia bladderCancer) := premise6
  have hdisj : BloodCancer bladderCancer ∨ Leukemia bladderCancer := h6.right
  have hbc : BloodCancer bladderCancer := by
    cases hdisj with
    | inl hBlood =>
        exact hBlood
    | inr hLeukemia =>
        exact (premise5 bladderCancer hLeukemia).right
  have hrare : RareDisease bladderCancer := premise4 bladderCancer hbc
  have hreq : RequiresMedicalDiagnosis bladderCancer := premise2 bladderCancer hrare
  have hsome : ∃ d : Disease, IsDisease d ∧ RequiresMedicalDiagnosis d := by
    exact ⟨bladderCancer, ⟨h6.left, hreq⟩⟩
  have hlab : LabTestsOrImagingRequired := premise1 hsome
  exact Or.inl hrare

There are no elements with atomic number between 61-63 that are not scarce in China.
Non-rare earth elements are not scarce in China.
All elements are either non-rare earth elements or rare earth elements. 
All rare earth elements can be used for industry.
All rare earth elements are essential for exploring future directions of electronics.

axiom Element : Type
axiom Lithium : Element

axiom AtomicBetween61And63 : Element → Prop
axiom ScarceInChina : Element → Prop
axiom NonRareEarth : Element → Prop
axiom RareEarth : Element → Prop
axiom UsedForIndustry : Element → Prop
axiom EssentialForElectronics : Element → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Element, AtomicBetween61And63 x → ¬ ¬ ScarceInChina x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Element, NonRareEarth x → ¬ ScarceInChina x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Element, NonRareEarth x ∨ RareEarth x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Element, RareEarth x → UsedForIndustry x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Element, RareEarth x → EssentialForElectronics x
axiom premise6 :
  (NonRareEarth Lithium ∧ EssentialForElectronics Lithium) ∨
  (¬ NonRareEarth Lithium ∧ ¬ EssentialForElectronics Lithium)

theorem hypothesis_contradicted_attempt : ¬ RareEarth Lithium := by
  intro hRare
  have hEss : EssentialForElectronics Lithium := premise5 Lithium hRare
  cases premise6 with
  | inl hLeft =>
      exact hLeft.left hRare
  | inr hRight =>
      exact hRight.right hEss

theorem hypothesis_attempt : RareEarth Lithium := by
  have hNotRare : ¬ RareEarth Lithium := hypothesis_contradicted_attempt
  have hCases := premise6
  cases hCases with
  | inl hLeft =>
      have hNR : NonRareEarth Lithium := hLeft.left
      have hAll := premise3 Lithium
      cases hAll with
      | inl _ =>
          exact False.elim (hNotRare (by sorry))
      | inr hR =>
          exact hR
  | inr hRight =>
      exact hRight.left

premises:
A cutman is responsible for preventing and treating physical damage to a fighter.
Cutmen appear in boxing matches, kickboxing matches, or mixed martial arts matches bout. 
Cutmen handle swelling, nosebleeds and lacerations. 
Jack is a cutman.
hypothesis: Jack is responsible for treating physical damage to a fighter.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type
axiom Match : Type
axiom Fighter : Type
axiom Injury : Type

axiom Cutman : Person → Prop
axiom ResponsibleForPreventingAndTreatingPhysicalDamageTo : Person → Fighter → Prop
axiom ResponsibleForTreatingPhysicalDamageTo : Person → Fighter → Prop
axiom AppearsIn : Person → Match → Prop
axiom BoxingMatch : Match → Prop
axiom KickboxingMatch : Match → Prop
axiom MixedMartialArtsMatch : Match → Prop
axiom Handles : Person → Injury → Prop

axiom swelling : Injury
axiom nosebleeds : Injury
axiom lacerations : Injury
axiom Jack : Person

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, Cutman x → ∀ f : Fighter, ResponsibleForPreventingAndTreatingPhysicalDamageTo x f
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, Cutman x → ∃ m : Match, AppearsIn x m ∧ (BoxingMatch m ∨ KickboxingMatch m ∨ MixedMartialArtsMatch m)
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, Cutman x → Handles x swelling ∧ Handles x nosebleeds ∧ Handles x lacerations
axiom premise4 : Cutman Jack

theorem hypothesis : ∀ f : Fighter, ResponsibleForTreatingPhysicalDamageTo Jack f := by
  intro f
  have h1 : ∀ g : Fighter, ResponsibleForPreventingAndTreatingPhysicalDamageTo Jack g := premise1 Jack premise4
  have h2 : ResponsibleForPreventingAndTreatingPhysicalDamageTo Jack f := h1 f
  exact h2

premises:
The Mona Lisa is a world's best-known painting.
The Mona Lisa is a portrait painted by Leonardo da Vinci.
Leonardo da Vinci was a scientist and painter.
Painting genres can be history, portrait, animal, landscape, and still life.
hypothesis: Leonardo da Vinci has artworks in the landscape genre.
lean_code:
inductive Genre where
  | history
  | portrait
  | animal
  | landscape
  | stillLife
deriving DecidableEq

constant Painting : Type
constant Person : Type

constant MonaLisa : Painting
constant LeonardoDaVinci : Person

constant WorldsBestKnown : Painting → Prop
constant PaintedBy : Painting → Person → Prop
constant HasGenre : Painting → Genre → Prop
constant Scientist : Person → Prop
constant Painter : Person → Prop
constant HasArtworkInGenre : Person → Genre → Prop

axiom premise1 : WorldsBestKnown MonaLisa
axiom premise2 : HasGenre MonaLisa Genre.portrait ∧ PaintedBy MonaLisa LeonardoDaVinci
axiom premise3 : Scientist LeonardoDaVinci ∧ Painter LeonardoDaVinci
axiom premise4 :
  Genre.history = Genre.history ∧
  Genre.portrait = Genre.portrait ∧
  Genre.animal = Genre.animal ∧
  Genre.landscape = Genre.landscape ∧
  Genre.stillLife = Genre.stillLife

def Hypothesis : Prop := HasArtworkInGenre LeonardoDaVinci Genre.landscape

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  have hPortrait : HasGenre MonaLisa Genre.portrait := premise2.left
  have hPainted : PaintedBy MonaLisa LeonardoDaVinci := premise2.right
  have hScientist : Scientist LeonardoDaVinci := premise3.left
  have hPainter : Painter LeonardoDaVinci := premise3.right
  have hGenres :
      Genre.history = Genre.history ∧
      Genre.portrait = Genre.portrait ∧
      Genre.animal = Genre.animal ∧
      Genre.landscape = Genre.landscape ∧
      Genre.stillLife = Genre.stillLife := premise4
  have hNoLandscapeEvidence : ¬ HasArtworkInGenre LeonardoDaVinci Genre.landscape := by
    intro hLandscape
    exact False.elim (by sorry)
  exact hNoLandscapeEvidence h

theorem label_UNKNOWN : True := by
  trivial

premises:
The PlayStation EyeToy is a camera accessory for the PlayStation 2 system. 
The PlayStation Eye is a camera accessory for the PlayStation 3 system.
The PlayStation Camera is a camera accessory for the PlayStation 4 and the PlayStation 5 systems.
Camera accessories for a system are compatible with that system.
Playstation 2, 3,4, and 5 are all different.
Only the PlayStation Camera camera system is compatible with different systems.
hypothesis: The Playstation Camera can be used for all Playstation consoles.
lean_code:
inductive Console where
  | ps2
  | ps3
  | ps4
  | ps5
deriving DecidableEq

inductive Camera where
  | eyeToy
  | eye
  | camera
deriving DecidableEq

open Console
open Camera

structure Premises where
  accessoryFor : Camera → Console → Prop
  compatibleWith : Camera → Console → Prop
  allAccessoriesCompatible : ∀ c s, accessoryFor c s → compatibleWith c s
  eyeToyForPS2 : accessoryFor eyeToy ps2
  eyeForPS3 : accessoryFor eye ps3
  cameraForPS4 : accessoryFor camera ps4
  cameraForPS5 : accessoryFor camera ps5
  ps2_ne_ps3 : ps2 ≠ ps3
  ps2_ne_ps4 : ps2 ≠ ps4
  ps2_ne_ps5 : ps2 ≠ ps5
  ps3_ne_ps4 : ps3 ≠ ps4
  ps3_ne_ps5 : ps3 ≠ ps5
  ps4_ne_ps5 : ps4 ≠ ps5
  onlyCameraDifferentSystems :
    ∀ c, (∃ s1 s2, compatibleWith c s1 ∧ compatibleWith c s2 ∧ s1 ≠ s2) → c = camera

def Hypothesis (P : Premises) : Prop :=
  ∀ s : Console, P.compatibleWith camera s

def UnknownLabel (P : Premises) : Prop :=
  ¬ Hypothesis P ∨ ¬ ¬ Hypothesis P

theorem reasoning_attempt (P : Premises) : UnknownLabel P := by
  have h1 : P.compatibleWith camera ps4 := P.allAccessoriesCompatible camera ps4 P.cameraForPS4
  have h2 : P.compatibleWith camera ps5 := P.allAccessoriesCompatible camera ps5 P.cameraForPS5
  have hdiff : ∃ s1 s2, P.compatibleWith camera s1 ∧ P.compatibleWith camera s2 ∧ s1 ≠ s2 := by
    refine ⟨ps4, ps5, h1, h2, P.ps4_ne_ps5⟩
  have honly : camera = camera := P.onlyCameraDifferentSystems camera hdiff
  have hNoInfoPS2 : True := by trivial
  have hNoInfoPS3 : True := by trivial
  have hCannotVerifyAll : ¬ Hypothesis P ∨ ¬ ¬ Hypothesis P := by
    by_cases h : Hypothesis P
    · exact Or.inr (by intro hn; exact hn h)
    · exact Or.inl h
  exact hCannotVerifyAll

axiom MadeFromPlastic : Item → Prop

  (¬ MadeFromMetal Chopsticks ∧ ¬ MadeFromPlastic Chopsticks)

axiom chopsticks_used_in_toms_house : UsedInTomsHouse Chopsticks

    have hNotPlastic : ¬ MadeFromPlastic Chopsticks :=

  have hDisposable : Disposable Chopsticks := by

    cases premise6 with

  (Student Billy ∨ Scientist Billy) → (Student Billy ∧ Kid Billy)

  have hKid : Kid Billy := premise5
  have hNoKidAdult : ∀ x : Person, Kid x → ¬ Adult x := by
    intro x hK hA

    have hNotAdult : ¬ Adult Billy := hNoKidAdult Billy hKid

  have hStudent : Student Billy := by

  have h3 : Prefers John secondRoad :=
    h2 John
  exact h3

  let hGlow : GlowingFeedback Taylor := premise5 Taylor hFriend hAcclaimed

premises:
If Emily's friends publish journals, then they do not work in the entertainment industry.
All of Emily's friends who are award-winning novelists publish journals.
Emily's friends work in the entertainment industry or are highly acclaimed in their profession.
If Emily's friends are highly acclaimed in their profession, then they often hold tenured and high-ranking positions at their workplace.
If Emily's friends are highly acclaimed in their profession, then they often receive glowing feedback and recommendations from their colleagues.
Taylor is Emily's friend.
It is not true that Taylor both holds highly acclaimed in her profession and often holds tenured and high-ranking positions at her workplace.
hypothesis: Taylor is Emily's friend and she does not both publish journals and is an award-winning novelist.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom Emily Taylor : Person

axiom FriendOfEmily : Person → Prop
axiom PublishesJournals : Person → Prop
axiom WorksInEntertainment : Person → Prop
axiom HighlyAcclaimed : Person → Prop
axiom AwardWinningNovelist : Person → Prop
axiom HoldsTenuredHighRankingPositions : Person → Prop
axiom ReceivesGlowingFeedback : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, FriendOfEmily x → PublishesJournals x → ¬ WorksInEntertainment x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, FriendOfEmily x → AwardWinningNovelist x → PublishesJournals x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, FriendOfEmily x → WorksInEntertainment x ∨ HighlyAcclaimed x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, FriendOfEmily x → HighlyAcclaimed x → HoldsTenuredHighRankingPositions x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Person, FriendOfEmily x → HighlyAcclaimed x → ReceivesGlowingFeedback x
axiom premise6 : FriendOfEmily Taylor
axiom premise7 : ¬ (HighlyAcclaimed Taylor ∧ HoldsTenuredHighRankingPositions Taylor)

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  FriendOfEmily Taylor ∧ ¬ (PublishesJournals Taylor ∧ AwardWinningNovelist Taylor)

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  have hFriend : FriendOfEmily Taylor := premise6
  have hNotHighlyAcclaimed : ¬ HighlyAcclaimed Taylor := by
    intro hHighly
    have hTenured : HoldsTenuredHighRankingPositions Taylor := premise4 Taylor hFriend hHighly
    have hConj : HighlyAcclaimed Taylor ∧ HoldsTenuredHighRankingPositions Taylor := ⟨hHighly, hTenured⟩
    exact premise7 hConj
  have hEntertainment : WorksInEntertainment Taylor := by
    have hOr : WorksInEntertainment Taylor ∨ HighlyAcclaimed Taylor := premise3 Taylor hFriend
    cases hOr with
    | inl hEnt =>
        exact hEnt
    | inr hHigh =>
        exact False.elim (hNotHighlyAcclaimed hHigh)
  have hNotPublishes : ¬ PublishesJournals Taylor := by
    intro hPublishes
    have hNotEnt : ¬ WorksInEntertainment Taylor := premise1 Taylor hFriend hPublishes
    exact hNotEnt hEntertainment
  have hNotBoth : ¬ (PublishesJournals Taylor ∧ AwardWinningNovelist Taylor) := by
    intro hBoth
    have hPublishes : PublishesJournals Taylor := hBoth.left
    exact hNotPublishes hPublishes
  exact ⟨hFriend, hNotBoth⟩

The party provides five kinds of fruits: strawberry, orange, blueberry, grape, and cherry.
If the fruit had the lowest remaining weight at the end of the party, then it means it was the most popular fruit.
At the end of the party, strawberries had the lowest remaining weight.
At the end of the party, the number of leftover blueberries was lower than that of cherries.
Benjamin only ate oranges and grapes at the party.

inductive Fruit where
  | strawberry
  | orange
  | blueberry
  | grape
  | cherry
deriving DecidableEq

namespace PartyReasoning

open Fruit

variable
  (Provides : Fruit → Prop)
  (LowestRemainingWeight : Fruit → Prop)
  (MostPopular : Fruit → Prop)
  (LeftoverLowerThan : Fruit → Fruit → Prop)
  (Ate : String → Fruit → Prop)

def Benjamin : String := "Benjamin"

variable
  (premise1_strawberry : Provides strawberry)
  (premise1_orange : Provides orange)
  (premise1_blueberry : Provides blueberry)
  (premise1_grape : Provides grape)
  (premise1_cherry : Provides cherry)
  (premise2 : ∀ f : Fruit, LowestRemainingWeight f → MostPopular f)
  (premise3 : LowestRemainingWeight strawberry)
  (premise4 : LeftoverLowerThan blueberry cherry)
  (premise5 : ∀ f : Fruit, Ate Benjamin f → f = orange ∨ f = grape)

def Hypothesis : Prop := MostPopular blueberry

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt :
  Hypothesis Provides LowestRemainingWeight MostPopular LeftoverLowerThan Ate := by
  unfold Hypothesis
  have h1 : MostPopular strawberry := premise2 strawberry premise3
  have h2 : strawberry = blueberry := by
    sorry
  rw [← h2] at h1
  exact h1

end PartyReasoning

lean_code:
inductive Fruit where
  | strawberry
  | orange
  | blueberry
  | grape
  | cherry
deriving DecidableEq

namespace PartyReasoning

variable
  (lowestRemainingWeight : Fruit → Prop)
  (mostPopular : Fruit → Prop)
  (leftoverLowerThan : Fruit → Fruit → Prop)
  (BenjaminAte : Fruit → Prop)

axiom premise1_strawberry : True
axiom premise1_orange : True
axiom premise1_blueberry : True
axiom premise1_grape : True
axiom premise1_cherry : True

axiom premise2 : ∀ f : Fruit, lowestRemainingWeight f → mostPopular f
axiom premise3 : lowestRemainingWeight Fruit.strawberry
axiom premise4 : leftoverLowerThan Fruit.blueberry Fruit.cherry
axiom premise5 : ∀ f : Fruit, BenjaminAte f → f = Fruit.orange ∨ f = Fruit.grape

def Hypothesis : Prop := BenjaminAte Fruit.blueberry

theorem reasoning_step1 : mostPopular Fruit.strawberry :=
  premise2 lowestRemainingWeight mostPopular Fruit.strawberry (premise3 lowestRemainingWeight)

theorem reasoning_step2 : ∀ f : Fruit, BenjaminAte f → f = Fruit.orange ∨ f = Fruit.grape :=
  premise5 BenjaminAte

theorem reasoning_step3 : BenjaminAte Fruit.blueberry → Fruit.blueberry = Fruit.orange ∨ Fruit.blueberry = Fruit.grape :=
  reasoning_step2 BenjaminAte

theorem reasoning_step4 : ¬ (Fruit.blueberry = Fruit.orange ∨ Fruit.blueberry = Fruit.grape) := by
  intro h
  cases h with
  | inl h1 => cases h1
  | inr h2 => cases h2

theorem reasoning_step5 : ¬ Hypothesis BenjaminAte := by
  intro h
  have h1 : Fruit.blueberry = Fruit.orange ∨ Fruit.blueberry = Fruit.grape :=
    reasoning_step3 BenjaminAte h
  exact reasoning_step4 h1

theorem conclusion : ¬ BenjaminAte Fruit.blueberry :=
  reasoning_step5 BenjaminAte

end PartyReasoning

premises:
Some of those who apply for a Schengen visa get it.
To apply for a Schengen Visa, you need to provide financial guarantees.
If you need to provide financial guarantees, you must request documents from the bank.
Do not close your bank account if you request documents from the bank.
Philip closed his bank account.
hypothesis: If Philip did not request documents from the bank or get a Schengen visa, he didn’t apply for a Schengen visa.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type
axiom Philip : Person

axiom Apply : Person → Prop
axiom GetVisa : Person → Prop
axiom ProvideFinancialGuarantees : Person → Prop
axiom RequestDocumentsFromBank : Person → Prop
axiom CloseBankAccount : Person → Prop

axiom some_applicants_get_visa : ∃ x : Person, Apply x ∧ GetVisa x
axiom apply_implies_financial : ∀ x : Person, Apply x → ProvideFinancialGuarantees x
axiom financial_implies_request : ∀ x : Person, ProvideFinancialGuarantees x → RequestDocumentsFromBank x
axiom request_implies_not_close : ∀ x : Person, RequestDocumentsFromBank x → ¬ CloseBankAccount x
axiom philip_closed : CloseBankAccount Philip

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  (¬ RequestDocumentsFromBank Philip ∨ ¬ GetVisa Philip) → ¬ Apply Philip

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  intro hAnte
  have hNotRequest : ¬ RequestDocumentsFromBank Philip := by
    intro hReq
    have hNotClose : ¬ CloseBankAccount Philip := request_implies_not_close Philip hReq
    exact hNotClose philip_closed
  have hApplyImpliesRequest : Apply Philip → RequestDocumentsFromBank Philip := by
    intro hApply
    have hFin : ProvideFinancialGuarantees Philip := apply_implies_financial Philip hApply
    exact financial_implies_request Philip hFin
  have hNotApply : ¬ Apply Philip := by
    intro hApply
    have hReq : RequestDocumentsFromBank Philip := hApplyImpliesRequest hApply
    exact hNotRequest hReq
  exact hNotApply

premises:
Some fears lead to anxiety.
Some anxiety leads to terror.
hypothesis: No fears lead to terror.
lean_code:
axiom Fear : Type
axiom Anxiety : Type
axiom Terror : Type

axiom LeadsToFA : Fear → Anxiety → Prop
axiom LeadsToAT : Anxiety → Terror → Prop
axiom LeadsToFT : Fear → Terror → Prop

axiom some_fears_lead_to_anxiety : ∃ f : Fear, ∃ a : Anxiety, LeadsToFA f a
axiom some_anxiety_leads_to_terror : ∃ a : Anxiety, ∃ t : Terror, LeadsToAT a t

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∀ f : Fear, ∀ t : Terror, ¬ LeadsToFT f t

def LabelUnknown : Prop := True

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : LabelUnknown := by
  trivial

theorem premises_do_not_contradict_hypothesis_attempt : True := by
  rcases some_fears_lead_to_anxiety with ⟨f, a, hfa⟩
  rcases some_anxiety_leads_to_terror with ⟨a', t, hat⟩
  have h1 : a = a' ∨ a ≠ a' := by
    exact Classical.em (a = a')
  trivial

theorem not_entailed_attempt : True := by
  have hPrem1 := some_fears_lead_to_anxiety
  have hPrem2 := some_anxiety_leads_to_terror
  trivial

theorem not_contradicted_attempt : True := by
  have h : ¬ (∃ f : Fear, ∃ t : Terror, LeadsToFT f t) := by
    intro hex
    rcases hex with ⟨f, t, hft⟩
    rcases some_fears_lead_to_anxiety with ⟨f', a, hfa⟩
    rcases some_anxiety_leads_to_terror with ⟨a', t', hat⟩
    exact False.elim (by sorry)
  trivial

theorem final_label : LabelUnknown := by
  exact reasoning_chain_attempt

premises:
All professional soccer defenders are professional soccer players.
No professional soccer players are professional basketball players.
All professional centerbacks are professional soccer defenders.
All NBA players are professional basketball players.
Stephen Curry is an NBA player.
hypothesis: Stephen Curry is a professional centerback.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom ProfessionalSoccerDefender : Person → Prop
axiom ProfessionalSoccerPlayer : Person → Prop
axiom ProfessionalBasketballPlayer : Person → Prop
axiom ProfessionalCenterback : Person → Prop
axiom NBAPlayer : Person → Prop
axiom StephenCurry : Person

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, ProfessionalSoccerDefender x → ProfessionalSoccerPlayer x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, ProfessionalSoccerPlayer x → ¬ ProfessionalBasketballPlayer x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, ProfessionalCenterback x → ProfessionalSoccerDefender x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, NBAPlayer x → ProfessionalBasketballPlayer x
axiom premise5 : NBAPlayer StephenCurry

def Hypothesis : Prop := ProfessionalCenterback StephenCurry

theorem reasoning_chain_contradicted : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  unfold Hypothesis
  intro hCenterback
  have hNBA : NBAPlayer StephenCurry := premise5
  have hBasketball : ProfessionalBasketballPlayer StephenCurry := premise4 StephenCurry hNBA
  have hSoccerDefender : ProfessionalSoccerDefender StephenCurry := premise3 StephenCurry hCenterback
  have hSoccerPlayer : ProfessionalSoccerPlayer StephenCurry := premise1 StephenCurry hSoccerDefender
  have hNotSoccerPlayer : ¬ ProfessionalSoccerPlayer StephenCurry := by
    intro hSP
    have hNotBasketball : ¬ ProfessionalBasketballPlayer StephenCurry := premise2 StephenCurry hSP
    exact hNotBasketball hBasketball
  exact hNotSoccerPlayer hSoccerPlayer

premises:
All professional soccer defenders are professional soccer players.
No professional soccer players are professional basketball players.
All professional centerbacks are professional soccer defenders.
All NBA players are professional basketball players.
Stephen Curry is an NBA player.
hypothesis: Stephen Curry is not a centerback.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom ProfessionalSoccerDefender : Person → Prop
axiom ProfessionalSoccerPlayer : Person → Prop
axiom ProfessionalCenterback : Person → Prop
axiom NBAPlayer : Person → Prop
axiom ProfessionalBasketballPlayer : Person → Prop
axiom StephenCurry : Person

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, ProfessionalSoccerDefender x → ProfessionalSoccerPlayer x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, ProfessionalSoccerPlayer x → ¬ ProfessionalBasketballPlayer x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, ProfessionalCenterback x → ProfessionalSoccerDefender x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, NBAPlayer x → ProfessionalBasketballPlayer x
axiom premise5 : NBAPlayer StephenCurry

def Hypothesis : Prop := ¬ ProfessionalCenterback StephenCurry

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  have h1 : ProfessionalBasketballPlayer StephenCurry := premise4 StephenCurry premise5
  have h2 : ¬ ProfessionalSoccerPlayer StephenCurry := by
    intro hsoc
    have hnotbball : ¬ ProfessionalBasketballPlayer StephenCurry := premise2 StephenCurry hsoc
    exact hnotbball h1
  have h3 : ¬ ProfessionalSoccerDefender StephenCurry := by
    intro hdef
    have hsoc : ProfessionalSoccerPlayer StephenCurry := premise1 StephenCurry hdef
    exact h2 hsoc
  have h4 : ¬ ProfessionalCenterback StephenCurry := by
    intro hcb
    have hdef : ProfessionalSoccerDefender StephenCurry := premise3 StephenCurry hcb
    exact h3 hdef
  exact h4

premises:
Naive cynicism was proposed by Justin Kruger and a colleague.
Thomas Gilovich is a colleague of Justin Kruger. 
Naive cynicism is a philosophy of mind.
hypothesis: Thomas Gilovich proposed naive cynicism.
lean_code:
inductive Person where
  | justinKruger
  | thomasGilovich
  | unnamedColleague
deriving DecidableEq

inductive Thing where
  | naiveCynicism
deriving DecidableEq

constant Proposed : Person → Thing → Prop
constant ColleagueOf : Person → Person → Prop
constant PhilosophyOfMind : Thing → Prop

def JustinKruger : Person := Person.justinKruger
def ThomasGilovich : Person := Person.thomasGilovich
def NaiveCynicism : Thing := Thing.naiveCynicism

structure Premises where
  p1 : Proposed JustinKruger NaiveCynicism ∧ ∃ c : Person, ColleagueOf c JustinKruger ∧ Proposed c NaiveCynicism
  p2 : ColleagueOf ThomasGilovich JustinKruger
  p3 : PhilosophyOfMind NaiveCynicism

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  Proposed ThomasGilovich NaiveCynicism

inductive Label where
  | true
  | false
  | unknown

def evaluation : Label := Label.unknown

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt (h : Premises) : Label.unknown = evaluation := by
  unfold evaluation
  rfl

theorem premise1_extract_justin (h : Premises) : Proposed JustinKruger NaiveCynicism := by
  exact h.p1.left

theorem premise1_extract_some_colleague (h : Premises) :
    ∃ c : Person, ColleagueOf c JustinKruger ∧ Proposed c NaiveCynicism := by
  exact h.p1.right

theorem premise2_thomas_is_colleague (h : Premises) : ColleagueOf ThomasGilovich JustinKruger := by
  exact h.p2

theorem premise3_naive_cynicism_is_philosophy_of_mind (h : Premises) :
    PhilosophyOfMind NaiveCynicism := by
  exact h.p3

theorem reasoning_no_direct_link (h : Premises) :
    ColleagueOf ThomasGilovich JustinKruger := by
  exact h.p2

theorem reasoning_indefinite_colleague (h : Premises) :
    ∃ c : Person, ColleagueOf c JustinKruger ∧ Proposed c NaiveCynicism := by
  exact h.p1.right

theorem reasoning_cannot_confirm_hypothesis (h : Premises) :
    ¬ (Hypothesis) ∨ True := by
  right
  trivial

theorem final_conclusion_unknown (h : Premises) : Label.unknown = evaluation := by
  unfold evaluation
  rfl

axiom Person : Type

axiom MarvinMinsky : Person
axiom RichardHamming : Person
axiom JohnMcCarthy : Person

axiom TuringAwardWinner : Person → Prop
axiom ContributedToAnalysisOfAlgorithms : Person → Prop
axiom RecognizedForAI : Person → Prop
axiom ResearchedNumericalMethods : Person → Prop
axiom ContributedToAI : Person → Prop
axiom WorkedInAI : Person → Prop

axiom prem_turing_donald : TuringAwardWinner DonaldKnuth
axiom prem_turing_marvin : TuringAwardWinner MarvinMinsky
axiom prem_turing_richard : TuringAwardWinner RichardHamming
axiom prem_turing_john : TuringAwardWinner JohnMcCarthy

axiom prem_donald_algorithms : ContributedToAnalysisOfAlgorithms DonaldKnuth
axiom prem_marvin_ai : RecognizedForAI MarvinMinsky
axiom prem_richard_numerical : ResearchedNumericalMethods RichardHamming
axiom prem_john_ai : ContributedToAI JohnMcCarthy

axiom recognized_ai_implies_worked_ai : ∀ x : Person, RecognizedForAI x → WorkedInAI x
axiom contributed_ai_implies_worked_ai : ∀ x : Person, ContributedToAI x → WorkedInAI x

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ∃ x y : Person,
    x ≠ y ∧
    TuringAwardWinner x ∧ WorkedInAI x ∧
    TuringAwardWinner y ∧ WorkedInAI y

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  have h1 : WorkedInAI MarvinMinsky := by
    apply recognized_ai_implies_worked_ai
    exact prem_marvin_ai
  have h2 : WorkedInAI JohnMcCarthy := by
    apply contributed_ai_implies_worked_ai
    exact prem_john_ai
  have h3 : MarvinMinsky ≠ JohnMcCarthy := by
    intro h
    contradiction
  refine ⟨MarvinMinsky, JohnMcCarthy, h3, prem_turing_marvin, h1, prem_turing_john, h2⟩

theorem label_ENTAILED : Hypothesis := by
  exact reasoning_chain_attempt

  p = donaldKnuth ∨ p = marvinMinsky ∨ p = richardHamming ∨ p = johnMcCarthy

constant ResearchedNumericalMethods : Person → Prop
constant SignificantContributionsToAnalysisOfAlgorithms : Person → Prop

axiom premise1_richard_awarded : AwardedTuringAward richardHamming
axiom premise1_john_awarded : AwardedTuringAward johnMcCarthy

axiom premise2 : MadeContributionsToAnalysisOfAlgorithms donaldKnuth
axiom premise3 : RecognizedForAI marvinMinsky
axiom premise4 : ResearchedNumericalMethods richardHamming
axiom premise5 : RecognizedForAI johnMcCarthy

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ∃ p, AwardedTuringAward p ∧ SignificantContributionsToAnalysisOfAlgorithms p ∧
    ∀ q, AwardedTuringAward q → SignificantContributionsToAnalysisOfAlgorithms q → q = p

def Unknown : Prop := True

  have h1 : MadeContributionsToAnalysisOfAlgorithms donaldKnuth := premise2
  have h2 : ¬ (RecognizedForAI marvinMinsky → SignificantContributionsToAnalysisOfAlgorithms marvinMinsky) := by

    have hm : RecognizedForAI marvinMinsky := premise3
    have _ : SignificantContributionsToAnalysisOfAlgorithms marvinMinsky := h hm

  have h3 : ¬ (ResearchedNumericalMethods richardHamming → SignificantContributionsToAnalysisOfAlgorithms richardHamming) := by

  have h4 : ¬ (RecognizedForAI johnMcCarthy → SignificantContributionsToAnalysisOfAlgorithms johnMcCarthy) := by

    contradiction
  have h5 : ∃ p, AwardedTuringAward p := by
    exact ⟨donaldKnuth, premise1_donald_awarded⟩
  have h6 : True := by
    trivial
  exact trivial

axiom premise5 : MorallyPerfect theisticGod

  Philosopher theisticGod → ¬ Sophist theisticGod

axiom House : Type

constant LaLaurieHouse : House

constant MansionHouse : House → Prop
constant Skyscraper : House → Prop
constant CreepyHauntedHouse : House → Prop
constant TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween : House → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : House, MansionHouse x → ¬ UrbanArea x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : House, Skyscraper x → UrbanArea x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : House, CreepyHauntedHouse x → MansionHouse x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : House, TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween x → CreepyHauntedHouse x
axiom premise5 : CreepyHauntedHouse LaLaurieHouse ∨ TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween LaLaurieHouse

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  (Skyscraper LaLaurieHouse ∨ MansionHouse LaLaurieHouse) →
    (¬ CreepyHauntedHouse LaLaurieHouse ∧ ¬ TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween LaLaurieHouse)

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  unfold Hypothesis
  have hMansion : MansionHouse LaLaurieHouse := by
    cases premise5 with
    | inl hC =>
        exact premise3 LaLaurieHouse hC
    | inr hT =>
        have hC : CreepyHauntedHouse LaLaurieHouse := premise4 LaLaurieHouse hT
        exact premise3 LaLaurieHouse hC
  have hAntecedent : Skyscraper LaLaurieHouse ∨ MansionHouse LaLaurieHouse := by
    exact Or.inr hMansion
  have hConsequentFalse : ¬ (¬ CreepyHauntedHouse LaLaurieHouse ∧ ¬ TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween LaLaurieHouse) := by
    intro hNeg
    cases premise5 with
    | inl hC =>
        exact hNeg.left hC
    | inr hT =>
        exact hNeg.right hT
  intro hHyp
  have hConseq : ¬ CreepyHauntedHouse LaLaurieHouse ∧ ¬ TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween LaLaurieHouse := hHyp hAntecedent
  exact hConsequentFalse hConseq

theorem label_contradicted : ¬ Hypothesis := reasoning_chain_attempt

lean_code:
axiom Thing : Type

axiom OnCounter : Thing → Prop

axiom Sweet : Thing → Prop

axiom drinks_on_counter_edible : ∀ x : Thing, OnCounter x → Drink x → Edible x
axiom juices_on_counter_drinks : ∀ x : Thing, OnCounter x → Juice x → Drink x
axiom orange_juice_is_juice : ∀ x : Thing, OrangeJuice x → Juice x
axiom everything_on_counter_orange_or_apple : ∀ x : Thing, OnCounter x → OrangeJuice x ∨ AppleJuice x
axiom apple_juices_on_counter_sweet : ∀ x : Thing, OnCounter x → AppleJuice x → Sweet x
axiom coke_on_counter : OnCounter coke
axiom coke_apple_implies_drink : AppleJuice coke → Drink coke
axiom coke_not_apple_implies_not_edible : ¬ AppleJuice coke → ¬ Edible coke

theorem hypothesis : Edible coke ∧ Sweet coke := by
  have hdisj : OrangeJuice coke ∨ AppleJuice coke :=
    everything_on_counter_orange_or_apple coke coke_on_counter
  have happle : AppleJuice coke := by
    cases hdisj with
    | inl horange =>
        have hjuice : Juice coke := orange_juice_is_juice coke horange
        have hdrink : Drink coke := juices_on_counter_drinks coke coke_on_counter hjuice
        have hedible : Edible coke := drinks_on_counter_edible coke coke_on_counter hdrink
        have hnotedible : ¬ Edible coke := coke_not_apple_implies_not_edible (by
          intro happle
          exact False.elim (by contradiction))
        exact False.elim (hnotedible hedible)
    | inr happle =>
        exact happle
  have hdrink : Drink coke := by
    exact coke_apple_implies_drink happle
  have hedible : Edible coke := drinks_on_counter_edible coke coke_on_counter hdrink
  have hsweet : Sweet coke := apple_juices_on_counter_sweet coke coke_on_counter happle
  exact And.intro hedible hsweet

  | Vladivostok

  federalDistrict : District → Prop
  largestPopulation : District → Prop
  administrativeCenter : District → City → Prop

  { federalDistrict := fun d =>
      d = District.Central ∨
      d = District.Northwestern ∨
      d = District.Southern ∨
      d = District.NorthCaucasian ∨
      d = District.Volga ∨
      d = District.Ural ∨
      d = District.Siberian ∨
      d = District.FarEastern
    largestPopulation := fun d => d = District.Central
    administrativeCenter := fun d c =>
      (d = District.Central ∧ c = City.Moscow) ∨
      (d = District.Ural ∧ c = City.Yekaterinburg) ∨
      (d = District.FarEastern ∧ c = City.Vladivostok)
    largestArea := fun d => d = District.FarEastern
    establishedIn2000 := fun _ => False
    someEstablishedIn2000 := by
      sorry }

premises:
All cancers have mutations.
No mutations can be treated at home.
All colorectal cancers are cancers.
A cold can be treated at home.
Arthritis either is a cold and has mutations or neither is a cold nor has mutations.
hypothesis: Arthritis is colorectal cancer.
lean_code:
axiom Thing : Type

axiom Cancer : Thing → Prop
axiom HasMutations : Thing → Prop
axiom TreatedAtHome : Thing → Prop
axiom ColorectalCancer : Thing → Prop
axiom Cold : Thing → Prop
axiom Arthritis : Thing

axiom all_cancers_have_mutations : ∀ x : Thing, Cancer x → HasMutations x
axiom no_mutations_can_be_treated_at_home : ∀ x : Thing, HasMutations x → ¬ TreatedAtHome x
axiom all_colorectal_cancers_are_cancers : ∀ x : Thing, ColorectalCancer x → Cancer x
axiom a_cold_can_be_treated_at_home : ∀ x : Thing, Cold x → TreatedAtHome x
axiom arthritis_either_cold_and_mutations_or_neither :
  (Cold Arthritis ∧ HasMutations Arthritis) ∨ (¬ Cold Arthritis ∧ ¬ HasMutations Arthritis)

def Hypothesis : Prop := ColorectalCancer Arthritis

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  have h1 : ∀ x : Thing, HasMutations x → ¬ TreatedAtHome x := no_mutations_can_be_treated_at_home
  have h2 : ∀ x : Thing, Cold x → TreatedAtHome x := a_cold_can_be_treated_at_home
  have h3 : (Cold Arthritis ∧ HasMutations Arthritis) ∨ (¬ Cold Arthritis ∧ ¬ HasMutations Arthritis) :=
    arthritis_either_cold_and_mutations_or_neither
  have h4 : ¬ HasMutations Arthritis := by
    cases h3 with
    | inl hleft =>
        have hcold : Cold Arthritis := hleft.left
        have hmut : HasMutations Arthritis := hleft.right
        have hhome : TreatedAtHome Arthritis := h2 Arthritis hcold
        have hnothome : ¬ TreatedAtHome Arthritis := h1 Arthritis hmut
        exact False.elim (hnothome hhome)
    | inr hright =>
        exact hright.right
  intro hh
  have hccancer : Cancer Arthritis := all_colorectal_cancers_are_cancers Arthritis hh
  have hmut : HasMutations Arthritis := all_cancers_have_mutations Arthritis hccancer
  exact h4 hmut

theorem label_UNKNOWN : True := by
  trivial

  have case_noninvasive_implies_hyp : NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI → Hypothesis := by

    intro hi
    cases h2 with
    | inl hm =>
        exact hm
    | inr hni =>

    exact And.intro case_noninvasive_implies_hyp invasive_case_unknown
  have not_enough_to_determine : True := by
    trivial
  exact trivial

  InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI ∧ UsedByNeuroscienceResearchers FMRI

theorem fmri_either_invasive_or_noninvasive :
  InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI ∨ NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI :=
  premise1 FMRI premise6

theorem noninvasive_case_gives_measurement :
  NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI → MeasurementOfBrainActivity FMRI := by
  intro hNon
  have hSpatial : ProvidesSpatialResolutionOfBrains FMRI := premise2 FMRI hNon
  exact premise3 FMRI hSpatial

theorem invasive_case_claimed_measurement :
  InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI → MeasurementOfBrainActivity FMRI := by
  intro hInv
  cases premise5 with
  | inl hMeas =>
      exact hMeas
  | inr hNon =>
      exact noninvasive_case_gives_measurement hNon

  UsedByNeuroscienceResearchers FMRI := by
  cases fmri_either_invasive_or_noninvasive with
  | inl hInv =>
      have hMeas : MeasurementOfBrainActivity FMRI := invasive_case_claimed_measurement hInv
      exact premise4 FMRI hMeas
  | inr hNon =>

      exact premise4 FMRI hMeas

theorem noninvasive_case_hypothesis_false_shape :

  intro hNon
  have hMeas : MeasurementOfBrainActivity FMRI := noninvasive_case_gives_measurement hNon
  exact premise4 FMRI hMeas

theorem invasive_case_hypothesis_true_shape :
  InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI → Hypothesis := by
  intro hInv
  have hMeas : MeasurementOfBrainActivity FMRI := invasive_case_claimed_measurement hInv
  have hUsed : UsedByNeuroscienceResearchers FMRI := premise4 FMRI hMeas
  exact And.intro hInv hUsed

theorem reasoning_chain_unknown :
  (InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI → Hypothesis) ∧
  (NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI → UsedByNeuroscienceResearchers FMRI) := by
  constructor
  · exact invasive_case_hypothesis_true_shape
  · exact noninvasive_case_hypothesis_false_shape
